steps.title: Track the pronoun antecedent
steps.content: The blank refers back to customers, the people whom cashiers asked about bags. Customers is plural and third person, so the pronoun must also be plural and third person.

steps.title: Match the pronoun form
steps.content: B uses one, a singular indefinite pronoun. C uses you, which shifts to second person and suggests the reader is the customer. D uses it, a singular pronoun that cannot refer to plural customers. The remaining pronoun agrees with customers in number and person. The answer is A.
